# Sub PEQ filters
# miniDSP-style peaking filters plus normalized biquad coefficients
# Biquad convention: y[n] = b0*x[n] + b1*x[n-1] + b2*x[n-2] - a1*y[n-1] - a2*y[n-2]

Filter 1: ON PK Fc 58.720 Hz Gain -9.000 dB Q 5.1050
  biquad b0=0.99959254474 b1=-1.99872217587 b2=0.999144391984 a1=-1.99872217587 a2=0.998736936724
Filter 2: ON PK Fc 80.144 Hz Gain -7.656 dB Q 7.1897
  biquad b0=0.99966816237 b1=-1.99883954811 b2=0.999198884679 a1=-1.99883954811 a2=0.998867047049
Filter 3: ON PK Fc 103.100 Hz Gain +2.566 dB Q 3.3374
  biquad b0=1.00029952362 b1=-1.99821180781 b2=0.997957778563 a1=-1.99821180781 a2=0.998257302188
Filter 4: ON PK Fc 28.941 Hz Gain -4.860 dB Q 2.1767
  biquad b0=0.999753494763 b1=-1.99884595051 b2=0.999096041615 a1=-1.99884595051 a2=0.998849536378

  biquad b0=1.00012217773 b1=-1.9993991339 b2=0.999285477883 a1=-1.9993991339 a2=0.999407655615

  biquad b0=0.999695808241 b1=-1.99839257569 b2=0.998784820379 a1=-1.99839257569 a2=0.99848062862
